---
title: "UNIONS-3500 Weak Lensing:<br>$B$-mode validation for cosmic shear"
occasion: Euclid Weak Lensing SWG telecon
description: "UNIONS-3500 B-mode validation for cosmic shear"
image: images/pure_eb_data_vector.png
author:
    name: Cail Daley
    affiliation: "CosmoStat, CEA Paris-Saclay  <br> <br> [on behalf of the UNIONS weak lensing team]{style=font-size:1.3em}"
date: April 27, 2026
date-format: long

format:
    revealjs:
        width:  1920
        height: 1080
        theme: [default, custom.scss]
        slide-number: true
        fig-align: center
        menu: false
        include-after-body: assets/no_footer_on_titleslide.html
        footer: Cail Daley | Euclid WL SWG telecon | April 27, 2026
        history: false
        hash: true
        pdf-separate-fragments: true
        pdf-max-pages-per-slide: 1
        template-partials:
            - title-slide.html
        center-title-slide: false

html-math-method:
    method: mathjax
    url: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mathjax@3/es5/tex-svg-full.js
include-in-header: assets/mathjax.html
---

## UNIONS is the first wide-field lensing survey in the northern sky

::: {.r-stack}
::: {.fragment .fade-out fragment-index=0 style="margin-right:-4%;margin-left:-4%"}
![](images/des_y6_footprints.png){width=108% fig-align="center"}
:::
::: {.fragment fragment-index=0 style="margin-right:-4%;margin-left:-4%"}
![](images/des_y6_footprints_unions.png){width=108% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="position:absolute;bottom:8px;right:6%;font-size:0.55em;opacity:0.7"}
Credit: DES Collaboration
:::
:::
:::

::: notes
Existing Stage-III cosmic shear surveys all live in the southern sky and overlap each other. UNIONS fills the north — zero overlap with DES, KiDS, or HSC. Different instrument, different Galactic foreground, different on-sky systematics. The only Stage-III survey that doesn't share sky with any of the others.
:::


## UNIONS-3500 at a glance

::: {style="margin-top:100px;font-size:1.05em"}
| | |
|:-|:-|
| **Imaging** | CFHT MegaCam $r$-band; $r_\mathrm{median} = 0.7''$ |
| **Effective area** | $2894~\mathrm{deg}^2$ after masking<br>(target $6250~\mathrm{deg}^2$) |
| **Source density** | $n_\mathrm{eff} \approx 5.0~\mathrm{arcmin}^{-2}$ |
| **Shape noise** | $\sigma_e \approx 0.27$ per component |
| **Shape measurement** | `ShapePipe` + metacalibration |
:::

::: {style="margin-top:80px;text-align:center;font-size:0.95em;opacity:0.85"}
Non-tomographic in this release; multi-bin calibration in preparation.
:::


## Paper II validates B modes for the UNIONS-3500 release

::: {style="margin-top:70px"}
- **Paper I** — Catalogues (Hervas-Peters et al.)
- **Paper II** — $B$-mode validation (this work)
- **Paper III** — Configuration-space cosmology (Goh et al.)
- **Paper IV** — Harmonic-space cosmology (Guerrini et al.)
- **Paper V** — Simulations and validation (Hervas-Peters et al.)
:::

::: {style="margin-top:80px;text-align:center"}
Paper II sets the scale cuts adopted in Papers III and IV.
:::


## Cosmic shear produces $E$ modes; $B$ modes flag systematics

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:55%"}

::: {style="margin-top:120px;font-size:1.0em"}
- Scalar potential → curl-free shear field → only $E$ modes to leading order
- Higher-order lens-lens / source-clustering / IA terms produce $B$ modes orders of magnitude below current sensitivity
- At Stage-III, **$B$-mode power = residual systematics**
- Absence of $B$ modes does not rule out contamination
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:45%"}

::: {style="margin-top:30px;text-align:center"}
![](images/emode_pattern.jpg){width=62%}

::: {style="font-size:0.8em;margin-top:-6px;margin-bottom:14px"}
$E$ modes (gradient-like)
:::

![](images/bmode_pattern.jpg){width=62%}

::: {style="font-size:0.8em;margin-top:-6px"}
$B$ modes (curl-like)
:::
:::

:::
::::::::::::::


## Stage-III B-mode failures trace detector, PSF, and astrometric effects

::: {style="margin-top:90px;font-size:1.05em"}
- **Repeating additive shear bias** — CFHTLenS, KiDS-450, DES-SV [[Asgari+19](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.02353)]
- **PSF higher-moment leakage** — HSC-Y3 [[Zhang+23](https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.03257)]
- **Astrometric residuals** — KiDS-Legacy [[Wright+25](https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.19441)]
- **Estimator-dependent passage/failure** — DES-Y3 / KiDS / HSC-Y3 under `HybridEB` [[Jefferson+25](https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.03964)]
:::

::: {style="margin-top:80px;text-align:center;font-size:0.95em;opacity:0.85"}
Each estimator weights angular scales differently — the same data can pass one null test and fail another.
:::


## Three Stage-III surveys see the same oscillatory $B$-mode pattern

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:62%"}

![](images/asgari19_allsurveys_EnBn.png){width=100% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.7em;text-align:center;margin-top:-6px"}
COSEBIs $E_n$ / $B_n$ for DES-SV, KiDS-450, CFHTLenS. Three angular ranges. From [Asgari+19](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.02353).
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:38%"}

::: {style="margin-top:140px;font-size:1.0em"}
- Three surveys, three pipelines
- $B_n$ panels (right): same coherent oscillation
- Survey $p$-values differ, the **shape** doesn't
:::

:::
::::::::::::::

::: notes
The B-mode panels on the right tell the story: a non-random oscillating residual that looks similar across DES-SV, KiDS-450 and CFHTLenS. The p-values disagree because covariances and areas differ — but the shape is shared. This is the signature we'll see in UNIONS too.
:::


## Mock systematics in COSEBIs each leave a distinct fingerprint

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:62%"}

![](images/asgari19_mocksys_EnBn.png){width=100% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.7em;text-align:center;margin-top:-6px"}
COSEBIs response to four mock systematics on KiDS-450. From [Asgari+19](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.02353).
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:38%"}

::: {style="margin-top:140px;font-size:1.0em"}
- Different systematics, different signatures
- **Regular pattern** (black): chaotic high-$n$ oscillations — matches the surveys' data
- High-$n$ COSEBIs carry the diagnostic
:::

:::
::::::::::::::

::: notes
"Regular pattern" is a c-term that repeats on focal-plane scales — the CCD-scale repeating additive bias mechanism. Compare the black stars to the previous slide's right column: same kind of signature. So the field has both the empirical pattern across surveys and a candidate mechanism that reproduces it in mocks. Hold this in mind when we look at the UNIONS COSEBIs.
:::


## We use three $E$/$B$-separable statistics with different filter supports

::: {style="margin-top:80px;font-size:1.05em"}
- **Pure-mode correlation functions** $\xi_\pm^{\mathrm{E/B}}(\theta)$ — integral transforms of $\xi_\pm$; deproject ambiguous modes [[Schneider+22](https://arxiv.org/abs/2110.09774)]
- **COSEBIs** $E_n, B_n$ — discrete orthogonal modes on $[\theta_\mathrm{min}, \theta_\mathrm{max}]$; deproject ambiguous modes [[Schneider+10](https://arxiv.org/abs/1002.2136)]
- **Catalog-based $C_\ell^{EE}, C_\ell^{BB}$** — spin-2 SHT at source positions; residual $E{\to}B$ leakage absorbed into $C_\ell^{BB}$ [[Wolz+25](https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21013)]
:::

::: {style="margin-top:70px;font-size:0.95em;text-align:center;opacity:0.85"}
Gaussian-only covariance (CosmoCov + NaMaster iNKA), validated on 350 GLASS mocks [[Tessore+23](https://arxiv.org/abs/2302.01942)] — PTEs are conservative.
:::


## Pure-mode $B$ modes show low-level structure outside the adopted cuts

![](images/pure_eb_data_vector.png){width=82% fig-align="center"}


## COSEBIs show a $>4\sigma$ first-mode excess — suppressed by the adopted cuts

![](images/cosebis_data_vector.png){width=62% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="margin-top:-12px;text-align:center;font-size:0.9em"}
Full range $1$–$250'$ (orange) vs. fiducial cuts $12$–$83'$ (blue). $n\leq 6$ carries the cosmological information.
:::


## $C_\ell^{BB}$ has a low-level offset but passes within the adopted range

![](images/cl_data_vector.png){width=68% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="margin-top:-8px;text-align:center;font-size:0.9em"}
$C_\ell^{BB}/\sigma$ (top), $C_\ell^{EB}/\sigma$ (bottom); gray shading marks $\ell = 300$–$1600$.<br>
$C_\ell^{EB}$ scatters around zero — any $B$-mode contamination is not strongly correlated with the lensing signal.
:::


## Only the fiducial catalog passes all three statistics

::: {style="font-size:0.9em;margin-top:30px;text-align:center"}
Four catalog variants from `ShapePipe` v1.4:
**initial** (loose size cut), **fiducial** (size cut $r_\mathrm{gal}/r_\mathrm{psf} > 0.707$), **masked** (adds star-halo masks), **relaxed-flags** ($+23\%$ density).
:::

![](images/pte_table_appendix_paper3.png){width=85% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.78em;text-align:center;margin-top:4px"}
Bold: PTE $< 0.05$ (null-test failure).
:::


## The adopted cuts sit in broad PTE-pass regions

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:38%"}

::: {style="margin-top:180px;font-size:0.95em"}
- $\chi^2$ vs. zero for each $(\theta_\mathrm{min}, \theta_\mathrm{max})$ pair
- Adopted cuts: $12$–$83'$, $\ell = 300$–$1600$
- Moving cuts several bins either way doesn't change the outcome
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:62%"}

![](images/pte_cosebis_talk.png){width=96% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.72em;text-align:center;margin-top:-6px"}
COSEBIs PTE vs. lower/upper scale cut. Square: adopted cuts.
:::

:::
::::::::::::::


## Filter functions, not basis, set sensitivity to contamination

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:46%"}

::: {style="margin-top:60px;font-size:0.98em"}
- COSEBIs can be computed from $\xi_\pm$ **or** from $C_\ell$ bandpowers via $W_n(\ell)$
- Both paths agree for all twenty modes
- $C_\ell^{BB}$ passes where COSEBIs computed from the same $C_\ell^{BB}$ fail
- The COSEBI filter functions concentrate sensitivity on the contaminated scales
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:54%"}

![](images/harmonic_config_cosebis_full.png){width=100% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.75em;text-align:center;margin-top:-6px"}
$E_n$ (top), $B_n/\sigma_n$ (bottom), from $\xi_\pm$ (circles) and $C_\ell$ (squares).
:::

:::
::::::::::::::

::: notes
Null tests in one representation cannot validate another — empirical support for Asgari+19 and Jefferson+25.
:::


## The oscillatory COSEBI pattern tracks CCD-scale repeating additive bias

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:48%"}

::: {style="margin-top:40px;font-size:0.98em"}
- Same signature in CFHTLenS, KiDS-450, DES-SV [[Asgari+19](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.02353)]
- A spatially varying $c$-term, fixed in focal-plane coordinates
- **CFHTLenS also used MegaCam** — points to a detector-level phenomenon, not a pipeline artifact
- MegaCam's 40 CCDs span $6$–$14'$; our lower cut of $12'$ excludes most CCD-scale pair separations
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:52%"}

![](images/cosebis_data_vector.png){width=100% fig-align="center"}

:::
::::::::::::::

::: notes
UNIONS dithers by ~1/3 field of view — larger than a single CCD — so each sky position averages the bias across several CCDs. This may attenuate but is unlikely to fully eliminate the pattern. Paper V identifies an additional pipeline-specific source: improper propagation of exposure offsets within shape measurement, which imprints a coherent additive shear at CCD scales.
:::


## Paper V identifies an additional pipeline-level source at CCD scales

::: {style="margin-top:90px;font-size:1.05em"}
- Multi-exposure fitting assumes objects centered on each exposure's pixel grid
- Consistent treatment would recenter using the WCS anchored at the tile-level detection position
- Relative pixel offsets between exposures in overlapping CCDs induce a preferred orientation → **coherent additive shear at the CCD scale**
- A fraction may be absorbed by multiplicative bias corrections; the adopted cuts remove the rest without fine-tuning
:::

::: notes
So we have two candidate sources for the CCD-scale repeating additive bias: the detector-level c-term mechanism identified by Asgari+19 across multiple Stage-III surveys, and this pipeline-level effect from improper exposure-offset propagation that Paper V has now identified. The adopted cuts remove both without needing to model either.
:::


## When the source can't be fixed, multi-framework cuts are the path

::: {style="margin-top:80px;font-size:1.05em"}
- UNIONS: systematic detected, source not yet localized — mitigated via scale cuts ($\theta_\mathrm{min}=12'$, $\ell_\mathrm{max}=1600$)
- A $B$-mode failure in any framework warrants scrutiny across all of them
- Different detectors and dithers, but the filter-function principle is unchanged
- Scale cuts that remove $B$-mode contamination also remove the correlated $E$-mode bias — no need to model the $E/B$ ratio
:::

::: {style="margin-top:60px;font-size:0.92em;text-align:center;font-style:italic;opacity:0.85"}
"As surveys become systematics-limited, disagreements between $B$-mode estimators<br>carry as much information as their agreements."
:::


## Summary

<ul style="margin-top:80px;font-size:1.05em">
<li style="margin-bottom:1.2em">UNIONS-3500 $B$ modes pass at the adopted cuts across all three statistics</li>
<li style="margin-bottom:1.2em">At full range, COSEBIs show an oscillatory pattern matching CFHTLenS-era repeating additive bias (same MegaCam)</li>
<li style="margin-bottom:1.2em">Only the fiducial (tightened-size-cut) catalog passes in every representation</li>
<li>Filter functions — not basis — set sensitivity; multi-framework validation is not optional</li>
</ul>


## Thank you

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:50%"}

::: {style="margin-top:140px;text-align:center"}
![](images/llm_collab_bow.png){width=92% fig-align="center"}
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:50%"}

::: {style="margin-top:200px;font-size:0.95em;line-height:1.45em"}
The analysis, the paper, and these slides
were produced through ongoing collaboration with Claude (Anthropic) and Codex (OpenAI).

<br>

I find it both possible and important to do thoughtful science this way.

<br>
<br>

[Paper II on arXiv soon — happy to discuss.<br>cail.daley@cea.fr]{style="font-size:0.92em;opacity:0.85"}
:::

:::
::::::::::::::

::: notes
The image and the message are deliberate. None of the prose in the paper, none of the analysis code, and none of these slides was typed by me directly — all of it was produced through close collaboration with Claude (left) and Codex (right). The work is mine in the sense that I directed it, made the scientific judgments, and own the conclusions. But the literal production happened in dialogue with these systems. I think we will look back on this period of cosmology as one in which a different mode of doing scientific work became viable, and I want to be transparent about it.
:::


## Backup {background-color="black" style="color:white" visibility="uncounted"}


## Ambiguous modes arise on any finite interval {visibility="uncounted"}

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:50%"}

::: {style="margin-top:80px;font-size:0.98em"}
On a finite angular interval, shear patterns with functional forms
$$\xi_+^{\mathrm{amb}}(\theta) = a + b\theta^2, \quad \xi_-^{\mathrm{amb}}(\theta) = c\theta^{-2} + d\theta^{-4}$$
cannot be assigned pure $E$- or $B$-mode origin.

<br>

Pure-mode and COSEBI filters deproject these modes; the catalog-based $C_\ell$ estimator absorbs residual mask-induced leakage into $C_\ell^{BB}$.
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:50%"}
![](images/emode_pattern.jpg){width=75%}
![](images/bmode_pattern.jpg){width=75%}
:::
::::::::::::::


## Configuration-space PTE maps by catalog version {visibility="uncounted"}

![](images/config_space_pte_fiducial.png){width=95% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.8em;text-align:center"}
$\xi_+^B$, $\xi_-^B$, and COSEBI $B_n$ PTEs vs. angular scale cuts, fiducial catalog. Black squares: adopted cuts.
:::


## Covariance: semi-analytic + MC propagation {visibility="uncounted"}

::: {style="margin-top:80px"}
- **Harmonic-space** ($C_\ell^{BB}$, $C_\ell^{EB}$): Gaussian covariance from NaMaster's iNKA framework
- **Configuration-space** (pure-mode, COSEBIs): `CosmoCov` covariance of $\xi_\pm$, propagated through the filter transforms
- COSEBIs: linear transformation $\mathbf{C}_{B_n B_m} = \mathbf{T}^{\mathsf{T}} \mathbf{C}_{\xi} \mathbf{T}$
- Pure-mode: 2000 MC realizations of $\xi_\pm$, empirical covariance of the transformed data vector
- All $B$-mode null tests use Gaussian-only covariance (conservative: no trispectrum, no super-sample terms)
- Validated on 350 GLASS mocks [[Tessore+23](https://arxiv.org/abs/2302.01942)]: all three statistics recover $B$ modes consistent with zero
:::


## COSEBI mock validation {visibility="uncounted"}

::: {style="margin-top:80px"}
- 350 lognormal GLASS catalogs [[Tessore+23](https://arxiv.org/abs/2302.01942)] at Planck 2018 cosmology, UNIONS footprint + galaxy positions + weights
- No PSF-related systematics — checks that the estimators don't generate spurious $B$ modes
- COSEBIs: mean $B_n$ biased by $< 0.3\,\sigma$ per mode
- Scatter comparable to fiducial error bars — uncertainty estimates of the right order
:::
